ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-16-2011 IN DEPT. 220, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE. THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PER CCP 36(A) CONDITIONED ON PLAINTIFF'S AGREEMENT THAT SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE HEARD ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR ESERVICE. TRIAL IS OCTOBER 3, 2011 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 220. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS SEPTEMBER 22, 2011. PLAINTIFF TO MAKE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 15, 2011. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 1:30PM ON SEPTEMBER 22, 2011. HAVING NO OBJECTION, THE COURT FURTHER ORDERED THAT THE PARTIES SHALL EXCHANGE BY FACSIMILE SERVICE A PARED DOWN LIST OF EXPERT WITNESSES NO LATER THAN 14 CALENDAR DAYS PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. AFTER PARTIES REVIEWED THE PROPOSED ORDER, ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (220)
REMOVED FROM ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE CALENDAR ON JUN-16-2011. ASSIGNED TO DEPT. 220 ON OCT-03-2011 AT 11:15 AM FOR TRIAL. (206)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Dec-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Meeti Sudame, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Sharon Gotautis, as wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor - in Interest to Stephen Gotautis, deceased; as Legal Heir of Stephen Gotautis, deceased ("plaintiffs").
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.

No appearance by defaulted defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company, in the amount of
$60,000.00 in Economic Damages,
$0.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $60,000.00.

The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.
